core_mechanism: reverse-mode automatic differentiation (the adjoint method) over a reversible forward propagation, in which the invertibility of the forward map lets intermediate states be reconstructed by running the dynamics backward instead of being stored, trading recomputation for an O(n_param) reduction in memory when accumulating gradients
brief_reason: The abstract builds a backpropagation gradient algorithm that 'exploits the reversibility' of the forward propagation to reconstruct rather than store intermediate states, cutting memory by O(n_param) versus conventional reverse-mode automatic differentiation.
